summary: Auto-Exit (Off-Hours) with configurable order type, Analysis Lab fixes, WebView2 cache-busting.
release_notes: New Auto-Exit (Off-Hours) feature — a software-based watchdog that monitors live bid/ask and fires an exit when your offset from fill price is hit, covering the gap where broker stop-loss orders don't work outside regular trading hours.
Configurable exit order type: choose between MKT (market) or LMT (limit with adjustable offset from bid/ask), matching the same pattern as close-position settings.
Disabled by default in Settings → Trading. When enabled, the Auto-Exit row only appears on the trading terminal when the market is closed and you have an open position.
Configurable offset from fill price, cancel-open-orders toggle, and ARM/DISARM per position with pulsing visual indicator.
Also accessible from the quick-settings gear popup on entry buttons for fast configuration.
Clear warnings throughout the UI that this is a software stop requiring BlitzTrade to be running — not a broker-held order.
Analysis Lab: fixed Run Query button not responding in pywebview — rewired via document-level event delegation.
Added no-cache headers and cache-busting query params to prevent WebView2/Edge from serving stale analytics pages.
